mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly change in industrial production signals expanding output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot INDPRO=107.1843 (above 100) indicates expansion into 2021
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change vs 20-day forward return is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the sign of correlation is negative in more than 40% of overlapping windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below the critical threshold of 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot UNRATE=6.2%, just above threshold, suggesting imminent crossing
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return following UNRATE falls below 6.0% is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the strategy’s Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in real GDP and nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based economic expansion with rising aggregate demand and labour income, which directly elevates equity valuations over a quarter horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive quarterly growth in GDPC1 and PAYEMS fails to produce median forward 60-day return above zero, or the correlation between the joint signal and forward returns is negative over any full market cycle.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate effectively at zero, a sustained increase in consumer price inflation above 2% year-over-year raises market expectations of future tightening, which reduces present equity valuations via higher discount rates, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot FEDFUNDS=0.08 (near zero), CPIAUCSL=263.161 (likely below 2% YoY pending calculation)
failure_condition: In episodes where CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the average 20-day forward return is not negative, or the trade fails to outperform a buy-and-hold in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly change in real retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly boosts revenues for many S&P 500 companies and elevates short-term equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The contemporaneous correlation between monthly change in RSAFS and forward 5-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.10 or the direction is negative in more than 60% of overlapping observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (more than 5%) signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, which acts as an early warning for economic slowdown and depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot HOUST=1580 (elevated, potential for mean reversion)
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return following a HOUST MoM decline >5% is not negative, or the signal’s precision (percentage of correct negative forecasts) is below 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices rise while the Federal Reserve keeps the policy rate anchored near zero, the resulting decline in real interest rates lowers the discount rate on equities, boosting S&P 500 valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL rises MoM and FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%, the forward 20-day return is not positive on average, or the strategy’s win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a phase of robust GDP expansion (YoY growth above 3%), a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals that the supply side is faltering, which typically precedes a broader economic contraction and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact: snapshot GDPC1=18783.9 (likely above pre-pandemic), INDPRO=107.18 (still expanding)
failure_condition: When GDPC1 YoY > 3% and INDPRO declines MoM, the median 60-day forward return is not negative, or the ratio of correct negative predictions is less than 60% over a full economic cycle.
